challenges.title: Implement Global Smooth Scrolling
challenges.description: Add smooth scrolling to a documentation-style page with anchor links. The page should have a table of contents with links to sections, and clicking each link should smoothly scroll to the corresponding section. Include proper scroll-margin for a fixed header.
challenges.hint: Apply scroll-behavior: smooth to the html element. Add scroll-margin-top to each section that matches your fixed header height.

Introduction

Welcome to the documentation. This page uses smooth scrolling for a polished experience.

Installation

Run npm install to get started.

Usage

Import the library and use it in your project.

API Reference

Full API documentation here.

challenges.expected_outcome: A documentation page with smooth scrolling anchor navigation. Sections scroll smoothly into view, and are offset by 80px to account for the fixed header.

challenges.title: Back-to-Top Button with Smooth Scroll
challenges.description: Build a 'Back to Top' button that appears when the user scrolls down 300px from the top. Clicking the button should smoothly scroll to the top of the page. The button should have a fade-in/out transition and be positioned in the bottom-right corner.
challenges.hint: Use window.scrollTo({ top: 0, behavior: 'smooth' }) for the scroll. Toggle a CSS class on the button based on scroll position using an intersection observer or scroll event.

starter_code.html: Long Article

solution.html:
How to Build a Back-to-Top Button

Section 1: Scroll down to see the button appear...

Section 2: The button will now be visible. Click it!

Section 3: Smooth scroll back to top in action.

challenges.expected_outcome: A back-to-top button that fades in after scrolling 300px, and smoothly scrolls to the top when clicked. The button has a polished transition effect.

challenges.title: Interactive Table of Contents with Active State Tracking
challenges.description: Build a floating table of contents sidebar that highlights the current section as the user scrolls. Clicking a ToC link should smoothly scroll to that section. The active state should update based on which section is in the viewport, using IntersectionObserver for performance.
challenges.hint: Use html { scroll-behavior: smooth } for click scrolling. Use IntersectionObserver to track which section is visible and update the active class. Use scroll-margin-top to offset for the ToC positioning.
